description: Gateway attB2 minimal attachment site; 25 bp, the specificity-2 attB recombination site appended to a DNA fragment through the attB2 PCR primer tail. It recombines with attP2 (BP reaction) to move the fragment into a pDONR entry vector. An engineered lambda-derived site; the canonical sequence is from Hartley, Temple & Brasch 2000.

references.authors: Hartley JL, Temple GF, Brasch MA
references.title: DNA cloning using in vitro site-specific recombination

label: Gateway attB2
note: Gateway recombinational-cloning attB site, specificity 2; 25 bp minimal attachment site appended to a DNA fragment via the attB2 PCR primer tail. The BP reaction (lambda Int + IHF) recombines attB2 with attP2 to insert the fragment into a pDONR entry vector. Engineered lambda-derived site; canonical sequence per Hartley, Temple & Brasch 2000.

provenance.sequence_source: Gateway attB2 minimal recombination site as defined in Hartley, Temple & Brasch 2000 (PMID 11076863) and the Gateway cloning manual; engineered site (no natural deposit) - sequence from the defining publication

functional_claims.label: Gateway attB2 is the minimal (~25 bp) specificity-2 attachment site appended to a PCR product via the attB2 primer tail; it recombines with attP2 (BP reaction, lambda Int + IHF) to insert the product into a pDONR entry vector.
attB2 carries the specificity-2 central overlap and recombines only with the cognate attP2, giving the orthogonality that makes Gateway cloning directional.
Added as a primer tail when amplifying a gene for Gateway entry: 5'-GGGGACCACTTTGTACAAGAAAGCTGGGT-[gene]; the 5' GGGG clamp protects the site during the recombination reaction.
